pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:0.3.eng: The chapter on offenses entailing expulsion

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:0.4.eng: The fourth training rule on expulsion

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.1.pli: Tena samayena buddho bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.1.eng: At one time when the Buddha was staying in the hall with the peaked roof in the Great Wood near Vesālī,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.2.pli: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā sandiṭṭhā sambhattā bhikkhū vaggumudāya nadiyā tīre vassaṁ upagacchiṁsu.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.2.eng: a number of monks who were friends had entered the rainy-season residence on the banks of the river Vaggumudā.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.3.pli: Tena kho pana samayena vajjī dubbhikkhā hoti dvīhitikā setaṭṭhikā salākāvuttā,
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.3.eng: At that time Vajjī was short of food and afflicted with hunger, with crops affected by whiteheads and turned to straw.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.3.comm: “Whiteheads” renders setaṭṭ(h)ikā, literally, “white bones”. Sp 4.403: Setaṭṭhikā nāma rogajātīti eko pāṇako nāḷimajjhagataṁ kaṇḍaṁ vijjhati, yena viddhattā nikkhantampi sālisīsaṁ khīraṁ gahetuṁ na sakkoti, “The disease called setaṭṭhikā means: an insect penetrates the stem, goes to the middle of the stalk, from the penetration of which the rice grains are not able to get sap.” This seems to be a description of so-called “whiteheads”, pale panicles without rice grains, caused by stem borers.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.4.eng: It was not easy to get by on almsfood.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.5.pli: Atha kho tesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ etadahosi—“etarahi kho vajjī dubbhikkhā dvīhitikā setaṭṭhikā salākāvuttā, na sukarā uñchena paggahena yāpetuṁ.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.5.eng: The monks considered the difficult circumstances, and they thought,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.6.pli: Kena nu kho mayaṁ upāyena samaggā sammodamānā avivadamānā phāsukaṁ vassaṁ vaseyyāma,
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.6.eng: “How can we have a comfortable rains, live in peace and harmony,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.10.eng: and they’ll support us in return.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.11.pli: Evaṁ mayaṁ samaggā sammodamānā avivadamānā phāsukaṁ vassaṁ vasissāma,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.14.eng: “There’s no need to work for the householders.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.15.pli: Handa mayaṁ, āvuso, gihīnaṁ dūteyyaṁ harāma,
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.15.eng: Let’s instead take messages for them,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.16.eng: and they’ll support us in return.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.17.pli: Evaṁ mayaṁ samaggā sammodamānā avivadamānā phāsukaṁ vassaṁ vasissāma,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.20.eng: “There’s no need to work or take messages for them.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.22.pli: Handa mayaṁ, āvuso, gihīnaṁ aññamaññassa uttarimanussadhammassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsissāma—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.22.eng: Let’s instead talk up one another’s superhuman qualities to the householders:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.31.eng: that monk has the three true insights,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.32.eng: and that the six direct knowledges.’

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.34.pli: Evaṁ mayaṁ samaggā sammodamānā avivadamānā phāsukaṁ vassaṁ vasissāma,
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.34.eng: In this way we’ll have a comfortable rains, live together in peace and harmony,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.36.pli: “Esoyeva kho, āvuso, seyyo yo amhākaṁ gihīnaṁ aññamaññassa uttarimanussadhammassa vaṇṇo bhāsito”ti.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.36.eng: This is the way to go.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.37.pli: Atha kho te bhikkhū gihīnaṁ aññamaññassa uttarimanussadhammassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsiṁsu—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.37.eng: Then those monks did just that.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.41.pli: “lābhā vata no, suladdhaṁ vata no, yesaṁ no evarūpā bhikkhū vassaṁ upagatā;
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.41.eng: “We’re so fortunate that such monks have come to us for the rainy-season residence.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.42.pli: na vata no ito pubbe evarūpā bhikkhū vassaṁ upagatā yathayime bhikkhū sīlavanto kalyāṇadhammā”ti,
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.42.eng: Such virtuous and good monks have never before entered the rains residence with us.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.43.pli: te na tādisāni bhojanāni attanā paribhuñjanti mātāpitūnaṁ denti puttadārassa denti dāsakammakaraporisassa denti mittāmaccānaṁ denti ñātisālohitānaṁ denti, yādisāni bhikkhūnaṁ denti.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.43.eng: And they gave such food and drink to those monks that they did not even eat and drink themselves, or give to their parents, to their wives and children, to their slaves, servants, and workers, to their friends and companions, or to their relatives.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.44.pli: Te na tādisāni khādanīyāni sāyanīyāni pānāni attanā khādanti sāyanti pivanti mātāpitūnaṁ denti puttadārassa denti dāsakammakaraporisassa denti mittāmaccānaṁ denti ñātisālohitānaṁ denti, yādisāni bhikkhūnaṁ denti.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.45.pli: Atha kho te bhikkhū vaṇṇavā ahesuṁ pīṇindriyā pasannamukhavaṇṇā vippasannachavivaṇṇā.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.1.45.eng: Soon those monks had a good color, bright faces, clear skin, and  sharp senses.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.1.pli: Āciṇṇaṁ kho panetaṁ vassaṁvuṭṭhānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ bhagavantaṁ dassanāya upasaṅkamituṁ.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.1.eng: Now it was the custom for monks who had completed the rainy-season residence to go and visit the Buddha.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.2.pli: Atha kho te bhikkhū vassaṁvuṭṭhā temāsaccayena senāsanaṁ saṁsāmetvā pattacīvaraṁ ādāya yena vesālī tena pakkamiṁsu.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.2.eng: And so, when the three months were over and they had completed the rains residence, those monks put their dwellings in order, took their bowls and robes, and set out for Vesālī.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.3.pli: Anupubbena yena vesālī mahāvanaṁ kūṭāgārasālā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.3.eng: When they eventually arrived, they went to the hall with the peaked roof in the Great Wood. There they approached the Buddha, bowed, and sat down.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.4.pli: Tena kho pana samayena disāsu vassaṁvuṭṭhā bhikkhū kisā honti lūkhā dubbaṇṇā uppaṇḍuppaṇḍukajātā dhamanisanthatagattā;
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.4.eng: At that time the monks who had completed the rains residence in that region were thin, haggard, and pale, with veins protruding all over their bodies.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.5.pli: vaggumudātīriyā pana bhikkhū vaṇṇavā honti pīṇindriyā pasannamukhavaṇṇā vippasannachavivaṇṇā.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.5.eng: Yet the monks from the banks of the Vaggumudā had a good color, bright faces, clear skin, and  sharp senses.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.6.pli: Āciṇṇaṁ kho panetaṁ buddhānaṁ bhagavantānaṁ āgantukehi bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ paṭisammodituṁ.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.6.eng: Since it is the custom for Buddhas to greet newly-arrived monks,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.7.pli: Atha kho bhagavā vaggumudātīriye bhikkhū etadavoca—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.8.eng: “I hope you’re keeping well, monks, I hope you’re getting by?

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.9.pli: kacci samaggā sammodamānā avivadamānā phāsukaṁ vassaṁ vasittha, na ca piṇḍakena kilamitthā”ti?
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.9.eng: I hope you had a comfortable and harmonious rains, and got almsfood without trouble?”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.10.eng: “We’re keeping well, sir, we’re getting by.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.11.pli: Samaggā ca mayaṁ, bhante, sammodamānā avivadamānā phāsukaṁ vassaṁ vasimhā, na ca piṇḍakena kilamimhā”ti.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.11.eng: We had a comfortable and harmonious rains, and got almsfood without trouble.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.12.pli: Jānantāpi tathāgatā pucchanti, jānantāpi na pucchanti …pe…
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.12.eng: When Buddhas know what is going on, sometimes they ask and sometimes not. They know the right time to ask and when not to ask. Buddhas ask when it is beneficial, otherwise they do not, for Buddhas are incapable of doing what is unbeneficial.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.12.comm: “Incapable of doing” renders setughāta, literally, “destroyed the bridge”. Sp 1.16: Setu vuccati maggo, maggeneva tādisassa vacanassa ghāto, samucchedoti vuttaṁ hoti, “The path is called the bridge. What is said is that there is the destruction and cutting off of such speech by the path.” The commentary seems to take setu, “bridge”, as a reference to the eightfold path. According to this understanding, the Buddha has cut off access to bad qualities, including bad speech, by fulfilling the eightfold path. I prefer to understand “bridge” as a metaphor for access, that is, the Buddhas no longer have access to what is unbeneficial.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.13.pli: dvīhākārehi buddhā bhagavanto bhikkhū paṭipucchanti— “dhammaṁ vā desessāma, sāvakānaṁ vā sikkhāpadaṁ paññapessāmā”ti.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.13.eng: Buddhas question the monks for two reasons: to give a teaching or to lay down a training rule.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.14.pli: Atha kho bhagavā vaggumudātīriye bhikkhū etadavoca—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.14.eng: And the Buddha said to those monks,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.15.pli: “yathā kathaṁ pana tumhe, bhikkhave, samaggā sammodamānā avivadamānā phāsukaṁ vassaṁ vasittha na ca piṇḍakena kilamitthā”ti?
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.15.eng: “In what way, monks, did you have a comfortable and harmonious rains? And how did you get almsfood without trouble?”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.16.pli: Atha kho te bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.17.eng: “But did you really have those superhuman qualities?”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.20.pli: “ananucchavikaṁ, moghapurisā, ananulomikaṁ appatirūpaṁ assāmaṇakaṁ akappiyaṁ akaraṇīyaṁ.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.20.eng: “It’s not suitable, foolish men, it’s not proper, it’s not worthy of a monastic, it’s not allowable, it’s not to be done.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.21.pli: Kathañhi nāma tumhe, moghapurisā, udarassa kāraṇā gihīnaṁ aññamaññassa uttarimanussadhammassa vaṇṇaṁ bhāsissatha.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.21.eng: How could you for the sake of your stomachs talk up one another’s superhuman qualities to householders?

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.22.pli: Varaṁ tumhehi, moghapurisā, tiṇhena govikantanena kucchi parikanto, na tveva udarassa kāraṇā gihīnaṁ aññamaññassa uttarimanussadhammassa vaṇṇo bhāsito.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.22.eng: It would be better for your bellies to be cut open with a sharp butcher’s knife than for you to talk up one another’s superhuman qualities to householders.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.24.pli: Tatonidānañhi, moghapurisā, maraṇaṁ vā nigaccheyya maraṇamattaṁ vā dukkhaṁ,
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.24.eng: For although it might cause death or death-like suffering,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.25.pli: na tveva tappaccayā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjeyya.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.25.eng: it would not cause you to be reborn in a bad destination.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.26.pli: Itonidānañca kho, moghapurisā, kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjeyya.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.28.pli: vigarahitvā dhammiṁ kathaṁ katvā bhikkhū āmantesi—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.2.28.eng: After rebuking them and giving a teaching, he addressed the monks:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.1.eng: “Monks, there are these five notorious gangsters to be found in the world.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.3.pli: Idha, bhikkhave, ekaccassa mahācorassa evaṁ hoti—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.3.eng: There is the notorious gangster who thinks like this:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.4.pli: ‘kudāssu nāmāhaṁ satena vā sahassena vā parivuto gāmanigamarājadhānīsu āhiṇḍissāmi
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.4.eng: ‘When the heck will I walk about in villages, towns, and royal capitals, with a following of a hundred or a thousand men,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.5.pli: hananto ghātento chindanto chedāpento pacanto pācento’ti.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.6.pli: So aparena samayena satena vā sahassena vā parivuto gāmanigamarājadhānīsu āhiṇḍati hananto ghātento chindanto chedāpento pacanto pācento.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.6.eng: Then after some time, he does just that.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.7.pli: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, idhekaccassa pāpabhikkhuno evaṁ hoti—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.7.eng: Just so, monks, a bad monk thinks like this:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.8.pli: ‘kudāssu nāmāhaṁ satena vā sahassena vā parivuto gāmanigamarājadhānīsu cārikaṁ carissāmi
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.8.eng: ‘When the heck will I walk about in villages, towns, and royal capitals, with a following of a hundred or a thousand people,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.9.pli: sakkato garukato mānito pūjito apacito gahaṭṭhānañceva pabbajitānañca, lābhī cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārānan’ti.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.9.eng: being honored, respected, and revered by both lay people and those gone forth, getting robes, almsfood, dwellings, and medicinal supplies?’

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.10.pli: So aparena samayena satena vā sahassena vā parivuto gāmanigamarājadhānīsu cārikaṁ carati sakkato garukato mānito pūjito apacito gahaṭṭhānañceva pabbajitānañca, lābhī cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānappaccayabhesajjaparikkhārānaṁ.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.10.eng: Then after some time, he does just that.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.11.pli: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, paṭhamo mahācoro santo saṁvijjamāno lokasmiṁ.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.11.eng: This is the first notorious gangster to be found in the world.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.12.pli: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco pāpabhikkhu tathāgatappaveditaṁ dhammavinayaṁ pariyāpuṇitvā attano dahati.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.12.eng: Or a bad monk learns the spiritual path proclaimed by the Buddha and takes it as his own.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.13.pli: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, dutiyo mahācoro santo saṁvijjamāno lokasmiṁ.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.13.eng: This is the second notorious gangster to be found in the world.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.14.pli: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco pāpabhikkhu suddhaṁ brahmacāriṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ carantaṁ amūlakena abrahmacariyena anuddhaṁseti.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.14.eng: Or a bad monk groundlessly charges someone living a pure spiritual life with a sexual offense.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.15.pli: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, tatiyo mahācoro santo saṁvijjamāno lokasmiṁ.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.15.eng: This is the third notorious gangster to be found in the world.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.16.pli: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco pāpabhikkhu yāni tāni saṅghassa garubhaṇḍāni garuparikkhārāni, seyyathidaṁ—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.16.eng: Or a bad monk takes valuable goods and requisites from the Sangha—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.17.pli: ārāmo ārāmavatthu vihāro vihāravatthu mañco pīṭhaṁ bhisi bibbohanaṁ lohakumbhī lohabhāṇakaṁ lohavārako lohakaṭāhaṁ vāsi parasu kuṭhārī kudālo nikhādanaṁ valli veḷu muñjaṁ pabbajaṁ tiṇaṁ mattikā dārubhaṇḍaṁ mattikābhaṇḍaṁ,
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.17.eng: a monastery, the land of a monastery, a dwelling, the site of a dwelling, a bed, a bench, a mattress, a pillow, a metal pot, a metal jar, a metal bucket, a metal bowl, a machete, a hatchet, an ax, a spade, a chisel, a creeper, bamboo, a reed, grass, clay, wooden goods, earthenware goods—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.17.comm: I have rendered muñja-reed and pabbaja-reed with the single word “reed”. I am not aware that these two kinds of reed can be distinguished in English. │ I render vihāra as “dwelling”, the idea that it is a monastic dwelling being implied. In later usage, especially in the commentaries, vihāra comes to refer to entire monasteries, rather than individual dwellings. The commentaries seem to agree that in its early usage the word refers to a dwelling. Sp 1.493: Vihāro nivesanasadiso, “A vihāra is like a house.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.18.eng: and uses them to bribe and create a following among householders.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.19.pli: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, catuttho mahācoro santo saṁvijjamāno lokasmiṁ.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.19.eng: This is the fourth notorious gangster to be found in the world.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.20.pli: Sadevake, bhikkhave, loke samārake sabrahmake sassamaṇabrāhmaṇiyā pajāya sadevamanussāya ayaṁ aggo mahācoro yo asantaṁ abhūtaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ ullapati.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.20.eng: But in this world with its gods, lords of death, and supreme beings, in this society with its monastics and brahmins, its gods and humans, this is the most notorious gangster of all: one who claims to have a non-existent superhuman quality.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.22.eng: Monks, you’ve eaten the country’s almsfood by theft.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.23.comm: This verse is also found at SN 1.35.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.24.eng: To be other than he truly is,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.26.eng: Like a cheater who has deceived.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.31.eng: It’s better to eat an iron ball,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.32.eng: As hot as a licking flame,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.33.eng: Than for the immoral and uncontrolled

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.35.pli: Atha kho bhagavā vaggumudātīriye bhikkhū anekapariyāyena vigarahitvā dubbharatāya dupposatāya …pe…
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.35.eng: After rebuking the monks from the banks of the Vaggumudā in many ways for being difficult to maintain, difficult to support …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.36.pli: evañca pana, bhikkhave, imaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ uddiseyyātha—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.36.eng: “And, monks, this training rule should be recited like this:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.37.1.pli: “Yo pana bhikkhu anabhijānaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ attupanāyikaṁ alamariyañāṇadassanaṁ samudācareyya—‘iti jānāmi iti passāmī’ti,
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.37.1.eng: ‘If a monk falsely claims for himself a superhuman quality, a knowledge and vision worthy of the noble ones, saying, “This I know, this I see,”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.38.pli: tato aparena samayena samanuggāhīyamāno vā asamanuggāhīyamāno vā āpanno visuddhāpekkho evaṁ vadeyya—‘ajānamevaṁ, āvuso, avacaṁ jānāmi, apassaṁ passāmi. Tucchaṁ musā vilapin’ti,
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.38.eng: but after some time—whether questioned or not, but having committed the offense and seeking purification—should say: “Not knowing I said that I know, not seeing that I see; what I said was empty and false,”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.39.eng: he too is expelled and excluded from the community.’”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.40.pli: Evañcidaṁ bhagavatā bhikkhūnaṁ sikkhāpadaṁ paññattaṁ hoti.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:1.3.40.eng: In this way the Buddha laid down this training rule for the monks.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.1.pli: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā bhikkhū adiṭṭhe diṭṭhasaññino apatte pattasaññino anadhigate adhigatasaññino asacchikate sacchikatasaññino adhimānena aññaṁ byākariṁsu.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.1.eng: Soon afterwards a number of monks, thinking they had seen and realized what in fact they had not, declared final knowledge because of overestimation.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.2.pli: Tesaṁ aparena samayena rāgāyapi cittaṁ namati dosāyapi cittaṁ namati mohāyapi cittaṁ namati.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.2.eng: After some time, their minds inclined to sensual desire, ill will, and confusion.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.4.eng: “The Buddha has laid down a training rule,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.5.pli: Mayañcamha adiṭṭhe diṭṭhasaññino apatte pattasaññino anadhigate adhigatasaññino asacchikate sacchikatasaññino, adhimānena aññaṁ byākarimhā.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.5.eng: yet we declared final knowledge because of overestimation.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.6.pli: Kacci nu kho mayaṁ pārājikaṁ āpattiṁ āpannā”ti?
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.6.eng: Could it be that we’ve committed an offense entailing expulsion?”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.9.pli: “Honti ye te, ānanda, bhikkhū adiṭṭhe diṭṭhasaññino apatte pattasaññino anadhigate adhigatasaññino asacchikate sacchikatasaññino adhimānena aññaṁ byākaronti.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.11.eng: And so, monks, this training rule should be recited like this:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.12.1.pli: “Yo pana bhikkhu anabhijānaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ attupanāyikaṁ alamariyañāṇadassanaṁ samudācareyya—‘iti jānāmi iti passāmī’ti,
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.12.1.eng: ‘If a monk falsely claims for himself a superhuman quality, a knowledge and vision worthy of the noble ones, saying, “This I know, this I see,”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.13.pli: tato aparena samayena samanuggāhīyamāno vā asamanuggāhīyamāno vā āpanno visuddhāpekkho evaṁ vadeyya—‘ajānamevaṁ, āvuso, avacaṁ jānāmi, apassaṁ passāmi. Tucchaṁ musā vilapin’ti,
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.13.eng: but after some time—whether he is questioned or not, but having committed the offense and seeking purification—should say: “Not knowing I said that I know, not seeing that I see; what I said was empty and false,” then,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.14.eng: except if it is due to overestimation,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:2.15.eng: he too is expelled and excluded from the community.’”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.4.eng: … The monk who has been given the full ordination by a unanimous Sangha through a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements that is irreversible and fit to stand—this sort of monk is meant in this case.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.6.pli: asantaṁ abhūtaṁ asaṁvijjamānaṁ ajānanto apassanto attani kusalaṁ dhammaṁ— atthi me kusalo dhammoti.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.6.eng: although a certain wholesome quality is non-existent in himself, not real, not to be found, and he does not see it or know it, he says, “I have this wholesome quality.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.8.pli: jhānaṁ vimokkho samādhi samāpatti ñāṇadassanaṁ maggabhāvanā phalasacchikiriyā kilesappahānaṁ vinīvaraṇatā cittassa suññāgāre abhirati.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.8.eng: absorption, release, stillness, attainment, knowledge and vision, development of the path, realization of the fruits, abandoning the defilements, a mind without hindrances, delighting in solitude.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.8.comm: “Delighting in solitude” renders suññāgāra abhirati, literally, “delighting in an empty dwelling”. According to the commentaries, this is often an idiom for solitude. MN-a 1.88: Tattha ca rukkhamūlānīti iminā rukkhamūlasenāsanaṁ dasseti. Suññāgārānīti iminā janavivittaṭṭhānaṁ; “And there rukkhamūlāni: by this is shown dwellings at the foot of a tree; suññāgārāni: by this (is shown) a place free from people.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.10.pli: te vā kusale dhamme attani upaneti attānaṁ vā tesu kusalesu dhammesu upaneti.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.10.eng: either he presents those good qualities as in himself, or he presents himself as among those good qualities.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.14.pli: yaṁ ñāṇaṁ taṁ dassanaṁ, yaṁ dassanaṁ taṁ ñāṇaṁ.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.16.pli: āroceyya itthiyā vā purisassa vā gahaṭṭhassa vā pabbajitassa vā.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.16.eng: announces to a woman or a man, to a lay person or one gone forth.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.17.eng: This I know, this I see:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.18.pli: jānāmahaṁ ete dhamme, passāmahaṁ ete dhamme atthi ca ete dhammā mayi, ahañca etesu dhammesu sandissāmīti.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.18.eng: “I know these qualities,” “I see these qualities,” “These qualities are found in me and I conform to them.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.20.pli: yasmiṁ khaṇe samudāciṇṇaṁ hoti taṁ khaṇaṁ taṁ layaṁ taṁ muhuttaṁ vītivatte.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.20.eng: the moment, the second, the instant after he has made the claim.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.22.pli: yaṁ vatthu paṭiññātaṁ hoti tasmiṁ vatthusmiṁ samanuggāhīyamāno—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.22.eng: he is questioned in regard to what he has claimed:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.30.eng: he is not spoken to by anyone.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.32.pli: pāpiccho icchāpakato asantaṁ abhūtaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ ullapitvā pārājikaṁ āpattiṁ āpanno hoti.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.32.eng: having bad desires, overcome by desire, claiming to have a non-existent superhuman quality, he has committed an offense entailing expulsion.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.34.pli: gihī vā hotukāmo upāsako vā hotukāmo ārāmiko vā hotukāmo sāmaṇero vā hotukāmo.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.34.eng: he desires to be a householder, a lay follower, a monastery worker, or a novice monk.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.35.eng: Not knowing I said that I know, not seeing that I see:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.36.pli: nāhaṁ ete dhamme jānāmi, nāhaṁ ete dhamme passāmi, natthi ca ete dhammā mayi, na cāhaṁ etesu dhammesu sandissāmīti.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.36.eng: “I don’t know these qualities,” “I don’t see these qualities,” “These qualities aren’t found in me and I don’t conform to them.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.37.eng: What I said was empty and false:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.38.pli: tucchakaṁ mayā bhaṇitaṁ, musā mayā bhaṇitaṁ, abhūtaṁ mayā bhaṇitaṁ, ajānantena mayā bhaṇitaṁ.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.38.eng: “What I said was empty,” “What I said was false,” “What I said was unreal,” “I said it without knowing.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.39.eng: Except if it is due to overestimation:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.40.eng: unless it is due to overestimation.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.42.eng: this is said with reference to the preceding offenses entailing expulsion.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.44.pli: seyyathāpi nāma tālo matthakacchinno abhabbo puna virūḷhiyā;
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.44.eng: just as a palm tree with its crown cut off is incapable of further growth,

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.45.pli: evameva bhikkhu pāpiccho icchāpakato asantaṁ abhūtaṁ uttarimanussadhammaṁ ullapitvā assamaṇo hoti asakyaputtiyo.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.45.eng: so too is a monk with bad desires, overcome by desire, who claims to have a non-existent superhuman quality not an ascetic, not a Sakyan monastic.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.46.eng: Therefore it is said, “he is expelled.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.48.pli: saṁvāso nāma ekakammaṁ ekuddeso samasikkhatā— eso saṁvāso nāma.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.48.eng: Community: joint legal procedures, a joint recitation, the same training—this is called “community”.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.49.pli: So tena saddhiṁ natthi. Tena vuccati— “asaṁvāso”ti.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:3.49.eng: He does not take part in this—therefore it is called “excluded from the community”.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.2.pli: jhānaṁ vimokkho samādhi samāpatti ñāṇadassanaṁ maggabhāvanā phalasacchikiriyā kilesappahānaṁ vinīvaraṇatā cittassa suññāgāre abhirati.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.2.eng: absorption, release, stillness, attainment, knowledge and vision, development of the path, realization of the fruits, abandoning the defilements, a mind without hindrances, delighting in solitude.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.4.pli: paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ catutthaṁ jhānaṁ.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.4.eng: the first absorption, the second absorption, the third absorption, the fourth absorption.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.6.pli: suññato vimokkho animitto vimokkho appaṇihito vimokkho.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.6.eng: emptiness release, signless release, desireless release.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.8.pli: suññato samādhi animitto samādhi appaṇihito samādhi.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.8.eng: emptiness stillness, signless stillness, desireless stillness.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.10.pli: suññatā samāpatti animittā samāpatti appaṇihitā samāpatti.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.10.eng: emptiness attainment, signless attainment, desireless attainment.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.14.pli: cattāro satipaṭṭhānā, cattāro sammappadhānā, cattāro iddhipādā, pañcindriyāni, pañca balāni, satta bojjhaṅgā, ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.14.eng: the four applications of mindfulness, the four right efforts, the four foundations for supernormal power, the five spiritual faculties, the five spiritual powers, the seven factors of awakening, the noble eightfold path.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.16.pli: sotāpattiphalassa sacchikiriyā, sakadāgāmiphalassa sacchikiriyā, anāgāmiphalassa sacchikiriyā, arahattassa sacchikiriyā.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.16.eng: realization of the fruit of stream-entry, realization of the fruit of once-returning, realization of the fruit of non-returning, realization of perfection.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.18.pli: rāgassa pahānaṁ dosassa pahānaṁ mohassa pahānaṁ.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.18.eng: the abandoning of sensual desire, the abandoning of ill will, the abandoning of confusion.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.20.pli: rāgā cittaṁ vinīvaraṇatā, dosā cittaṁ vinīvaraṇatā, mohā cittaṁ vinīvaraṇatā.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.20.eng: a mind without sensual desire, a mind without ill will, a mind without confusion.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.22.eng: because of the first absorption, there is delight in solitude;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.23.eng: because of the second absorption, there is delight in solitude;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.24.eng: because of the third absorption, there is delight in solitude;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.1.25.eng: because of the fourth absorption, there is delight in solitude.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.1.pli: Tīhākārehi paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjinti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.1.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I attained the first absorption,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.2.eng: before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.3.eng: while lying, he knows he is lying;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.4.eng: after he has lied, he knows he has lied.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.5.pli: Catūhākārehi paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjinti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.5.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I attained the first absorption,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when four conditions are fulfilled:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.6.eng: before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.7.eng: while lying, he knows he is lying;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.8.eng: after he has lied, he knows he has lied;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.9.eng: he misrepresents his view of what is true.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.9.comm: “Of what is true” is not in the Pali, but has been added for clarity.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.10.pli: Pañcahākārehi paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjinti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.10.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I attained the first absorption,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when five conditions are fulfilled:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.11.eng: before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.12.eng: while lying, he knows he is lying;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.13.eng: after he has lied, he knows he has lied;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.14.eng: he misrepresents his view of what is true; he misrepresents his belief of what is true.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.15.pli: Chahākārehi paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjinti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.15.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I attained the first absorption,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when six conditions are fulfilled:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.16.eng: before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.17.eng: while lying, he knows he is lying;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.18.eng: after he has lied, he knows he has lied;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.19.eng: he misrepresents his view of what is true;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.20.eng: he misrepresents his belief of what is true;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.21.eng: he misrepresents his acceptance of what is true.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.22.pli: Sattahākārehi paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjinti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.22.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I attained the first absorption,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when seven conditions are fulfilled:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.23.eng: before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.24.eng: while lying, he knows he is lying;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.25.eng: after he has lied, he knows he has lied;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.26.eng: he misrepresents his view of what is true;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.27.eng: he misrepresents his belief of what is true;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.28.eng: he misrepresents his acceptance of what is true;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.2.29.eng: he misrepresents his sentiment of what is true.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.1.pli: Tīhākārehi paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjāmīti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.1.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I’m attaining the first absorption,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.2.eng: before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.3.eng: while lying, he knows he is lying;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.4.eng: after he has lied, he knows he has lied.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.5.pli: Catūhākārehi paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjāmīti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.5.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I’m attaining the first absorption,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when four conditions are fulfilled:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.6.eng: before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.7.eng: while lying, he knows he is lying;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.8.eng: after he has lied, he knows he has lied;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.9.eng: he misrepresents his view of what is true.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.10.pli: Pañcahākārehi paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjāmīti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.10.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I’m attaining the first absorption,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when five conditions are fulfilled:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.11.eng: before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.12.eng: while lying, he knows he is lying;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.13.eng: after he has lied, he knows he has lied;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.14.eng: he misrepresents his view of what is true;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.15.eng: he misrepresents his belief of what is true.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.16.pli: Chahākārehi paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjāmīti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.16.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I’m attaining the first absorption,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when six conditions are fulfilled:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.17.eng: before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.18.eng: while lying, he knows he is lying;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.19.eng: after he has lied, he knows he has lied;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.20.eng: he misrepresents his view of what is true;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.21.eng: he misrepresents his belief of what is true;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.22.eng: he misrepresents his acceptance of what is true.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.23.pli: Sattahākārehi paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjāmīti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.23.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I’m attaining the first absorption,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when seven conditions are fulfilled:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.24.eng: before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.25.eng: while lying, he knows he is lying;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.26.eng: after he has lied, he knows he has lied;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.27.eng: he misrepresents his view of what is true;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.28.eng: he misrepresents his belief of what is true;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.29.eng: he misrepresents his acceptance of what is true;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.30.eng: he misrepresents his sentiment of what is true.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.31.pli: Tīhākārehi paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpannoti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.3.31.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I’ve attained the first absorption,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.4.1.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I attained the second absorption,” …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.1.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I attained the emptiness release,” …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.8.pli: appaṇihito vimokkho sacchikato mayāti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa …pe….

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.9.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I attained the emptiness stillness,” …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.16.pli: appaṇihito samādhi sacchikato mayāti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.17.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I attained the emptiness attainment,” …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.24.pli: appaṇihitā samāpatti sacchikatā mayāti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.25.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I attained the three true insights,” …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.31.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I attained the four applications of mindfulness,” …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.33.eng: “I attained the four foundations for supernormal power,” …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.38.pli: cattāro iddhipādā sacchikatā mayāti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.39.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I attained the five spiritual faculties,” …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.46.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I attained the seven factors of awakening,” …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.52.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I attained the noble eightfold path,” …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.58.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I attained the fruit of stream-entry,” …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.64.pli: arahattassa lābhimhi vasimhi arahattaṁ sacchikataṁ mayāti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.64.eng: “I’ve realized perfection,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.65.pli: Tīhākārehi rāgo me catto vanto mutto pahīno paṭinissaṭṭho ukkheṭito samukkheṭitoti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.65.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I’ve given up sensual desire,  renounced it, let it go, abandoned it, relinquished it, forsaken it, thrown it aside,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.66.pli: Tīhākārehi doso me catto vanto mutto pahīno paṭinissaṭṭho ukkheṭito samukkheṭitoti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.66.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I’ve given up ill will, renounced it, let it go, abandoned it, relinquished it, forsaken it, thrown it aside,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.67.pli: Tīhākārehi moho me catto vanto mutto pahīno paṭinissaṭṭho ukkheṭito samukkheṭitoti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.67.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “I’ve given up confusion, renounced it, let it go, abandoned it, relinquished it, forsaken it, thrown it aside,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.68.pli: Tīhākārehi rāgā me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇanti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.68.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “My mind is free from the hindrance of sensual desire,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.69.pli: Tīhākārehi dosā me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇanti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.69.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “My mind is free from the hindrance of ill will,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.70.pli: Tīhākārehi …pe… sattahākārehi mohā me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇanti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa āpatti pārājikassa—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.70.eng: If he lies in full awareness, saying, “My mind is free from the hindrance of confusion,” he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled … when seven conditions are fulfilled:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.71.eng: before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.72.eng: while lying, he knows he is lying;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.73.eng: after he has lied, he knows he has lied;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.74.eng: he misrepresents his view of what is true;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.75.eng: he misrepresents his belief of what is true;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.76.eng: he misrepresents his acceptance of what is true;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.77.eng: he misrepresents his sentiment of what is true.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:4.5.78.comm: “Basic series” renders, suddhika, which is a technical term used to create sections for long permutation series. See Appendix of Specialized Vocabulary for a detailed explanation.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.0.eng: Meaning to say first absorption, but saying something else

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.1.pli: Tīhākārehi paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjinti vattukāmo dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjinti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa paṭivijānantassa āpatti pārājikassa;
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.1.eng: If he lies in full awareness, meaning to say, “I attained the first absorption,” while actually saying, “I attained the second absorption,” then, if the listener understands, he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled;
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.1.comm: “If the listener understands” renders paṭivijānantassa. This is in accordance with Sp 1.219, which has this to say: Atha pana yassa āroceti, so na jānāti “ki ayaṁ bhaṇatī”ti, saṁsayaṁ vā āpajjati, ciraṁ vīmaṁsitvā vā pacchā jānāti, appaṭivijānanto icceva saṅkhyaṁ gacchati”, “When he who is informed does not understand, thinking, ‘What does he say?’ or he has doubt, or he understands after reflecting for a long time, then it is considered ‘one who does not understand.’” Grammatically paṭivijānantassa could refer to either the speaker or the listener (it can be regarded as a genitive agreeing with bhaṇantassa, thus referring to the speaker, or it can be regarded as a dative of the person spoken to, that is, the listener), but logically it seems it must refer to the listener. In accordance with common usage, “understanding” (paṭivijānantassa) must refer to understanding the overall meaning of what is said, not to knowing the exact words that have been spoken. Since the speaker knows he is lying, he understands the overall meaning. It follows that the understanding here must refer to the listener. A parallel construction is found at Bu Pj 1:8.4.10 where paṭivijānāti is used in connection with giving up the monastic training. Here the verb clearly refers to understanding on the part of the listener, that is, one has only succeeded in renouncing the training if the listener understands what one is saying. At Bu Ss 3:4.1.4 the same verb, this time in the aorist form paṭivijāni, again refers to the listener.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.2.eng: if the listener does not understand, he commits a serious offense when three conditions are fulfilled.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.3.pli: Tīhākārehi paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjinti vattukāmo tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjinti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa paṭivijānantassa āpatti pārājikassa;
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.3.eng: If he lies in full awareness, meaning to say, “I attained the first absorption,” while actually saying, “I attained the third absorption,” then, if the listener understands, he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.4.eng: if the listener does not understand, he commits a serious offense when three conditions are fulfilled.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.33.pli: mohā me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇanti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa paṭivijānantassa āpatti pārājikassa;
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.33.eng: “My mind is free from the hindrance of confusion,” then, if the listener understands, he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.34.eng: if the listener does not understand, he commits a serious offense when three conditions are fulfilled … when seven conditions are fulfilled:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.35.eng: before he has lied, he knows he is going to lie;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.36.eng: while lying, he knows he is lying;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.37.eng: after he has lied, he knows he has lied;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.38.eng: he misrepresents his view of what is true;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.39.eng: he misrepresents his belief of what is true;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.40.eng: he misrepresents his acceptance of what is true;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.41.eng: he misrepresents his sentiment of what is true.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.42.eng: The unconnected permutation series based on one item with a speech extension is finished.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.1.42.comm: The Pali text reads vatthu, “basis”, rather than vattu, “speech”. However, I have chosen to follow the alternative reading of vattu, found in the PTS edition, since this seems more reasonable to me given the context.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.2.0.eng: Meaning to say second absorption, but saying something else

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.2.1.pli: Tīhākārehi dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjinti vattukāmo tatiyaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjinti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa paṭivijānantassa āpatti pārājikassa;
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.2.1.eng: If he lies in full awareness, meaning to say, “I attained the second absorption,” while actually saying, “I attained the third absorption,” then, if the listener understands, he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.2.2.eng: if the listener does not understand, he commits a serious offense when three conditions are fulfilled.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.2.3.pli: Tīhākārehi dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjinti vattukāmo catutthaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjinti …pe…
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.2.3.eng: If he lies in full awareness, meaning to say, “I attained the second absorption,” while actually saying, “I attained the fourth absorption,” …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.2.4.pli: mohā me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇanti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa paṭivijānantassa āpatti pārājikassa;
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.2.4.eng: “My mind is free from the hindrance of confusion,” then, if the listener understands, he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.2.5.eng: if the listener does not understand, he commits a serious offense when three conditions are fulfilled.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.2.6.pli: Tīhākārehi …pe… sattahākārehi dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjinti vattukāmo paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjinti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa paṭivijānantassa āpatti pārājikassa;
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.2.6.eng: If he lies in full awareness, meaning to say, “I attained the second absorption,” while actually saying, “I attained the first absorption,” then, if the listener understands, he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.2.7.eng: if the listener does not understand, he commits a serious offense when three conditions are fulfilled … when seven conditions are fulfilled …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.2.8.eng: he misrepresents his sentiment of what is true.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.2.9.eng: The linked permutation series based on one item with a speech extension is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.2.10.eng: The basis in brief is finished.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.2.10.comm: “Basis” renders mūla. The mūla denotes a basic pattern to be followed in the succeeding permutation series.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.3.0.eng: Meaning to say he is free from confusion, but saying something else

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.3.1.pli: Tīhākārehi mohā me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇanti vattukāmo paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjinti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa paṭivijānantassa āpatti pārājikassa;
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.3.1.eng: If he lies in full awareness, meaning to say, “My mind is free from the hindrance of confusion,” while actually saying, “I attained the first absorption,” then, if the listener understands, he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.3.2.eng: if the listener does not understand, he commits a serious offense when three conditions are fulfilled.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.3.3.pli: Tīhākārehi …pe… sattahākārehi mohā me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇanti vattukāmo dosā me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇanti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa paṭivijānantassa āpatti pārājikassa;
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.3.3.eng: If he lies in full awareness, meaning to say, “My mind is free from the hindrance of confusion,” while actually saying, “My mind is free from the hindrance of ill will,” then, if the listener understands, he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.3.4.eng: if the listener does not understand, he commits a serious offense when three conditions are fulfilled … when seven conditions are fulfilled …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.3.5.eng: he misrepresents his sentiment of what is true.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.3.6.eng: The section based on one item with a speech extension is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.0.eng: Meaning to say any particular combination of individual attainments, but saying something else

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.1.pli: Yathā ekamūlakaṁ vitthāritaṁ evameva dumūlakādipi vitthāretabbaṁ.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.1.eng: The sections based on two items, etc., are to be given in detail in the same way as the section based on one item.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.2.0.eng: Meaning to say all the attainments but one, and instead saying the remaining one

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.2.1.eng: This is the section based on all items:

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.3.pli: Tīhākārehi …pe… sattahākārehi paṭhamañca jhānaṁ dutiyañca jhānaṁ tatiyañca jhānaṁ catutthañca jhānaṁ suññatañca vimokkhaṁ animittañca vimokkhaṁ appaṇihitañca vimokkhaṁ suññatañca samādhiṁ animittañca samādhiṁ appaṇihitañca samādhiṁ suññatañca samāpattiṁ animittañca samāpattiṁ appaṇihitañca samāpattiṁ tisso ca vijjā cattāro ca satipaṭṭhāne cattāro ca sammappadhāne cattāro ca iddhipāde pañca ca indriyāni pañca ca balāni satta ca bojjhaṅge ariyañca aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ sotāpattiphalañca sakadāgāmiphalañca anāgāmiphalañca arahattañca samāpajjiṁ …pe…
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.3.eng: If he lies in full awareness, meaning to say, “I attained the first absorption and the second absorption and the third absorption and the fourth absorption and the emptiness release and the signless release and the desireless release and the emptiness stillness and the signless stillness and the desireless stillness and the emptiness attainment and the signless attainment and the desireless attainment and the three true insights and the four applications of mindfulness and the four right efforts and the four foundations for supernormal power and the five spiritual faculties and the five spiritual powers and the seven factors of awakening and the noble eightfold path and the fruit of stream-entry and the fruit of once-returning and the fruit of non-returning and perfection …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.4.eng: and I’ve given up sensual desire …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.5.eng: and I’ve given up ill will …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.6.pli: moho ca me catto vanto mutto pahīno paṭinissaṭṭho ukkheṭito samukkheṭito, rāgā ca me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇaṁ dosā ca me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇanti vattukāmo mohā me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇanti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa paṭivijānantassa āpatti pārājikassa;
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.6.eng: and I’ve given up confusion, renounced it, let it go, abandoned it, relinquished it, forsaken it, thrown it aside; and my mind is free from the hindrance of sensual desire and my mind is free from the hindrance of ill will,” while actually saying, “My mind is free from the hindrance of confusion,” then, if the listener understands, he commits an offense entailing expulsion when three conditions are fulfilled;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.7.eng: if the listener does not understand, he commits a serious offense when three conditions are fulfilled … when seven conditions are fulfilled.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.8.pli: Tīhākārehi dutiyañca jhānaṁ tatiyañca jhānaṁ catutthañca jhānaṁ suññatañca vimokkhaṁ animittañca vimokkhaṁ appaṇihitañca vimokkhaṁ suññatañca samādhiṁ animittañca samādhiṁ appaṇihitañca samādhiṁ suññatañca samāpattiṁ animittañca samāpattiṁ appaṇihitañca samāpattiṁ tisso ca vijjā cattāro ca satipaṭṭhāne cattāro ca sammappadhāne cattāro ca iddhipāde pañca ca indriyāni pañca ca balāni satta ca bojjhaṅge ariyañca aṭṭhaṅgikaṁ maggaṁ sotāpattiphalañca sakadāgāmiphalañca anāgāmiphalañca arahattañca samāpajjiṁ …pe…
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.8.eng: If he lies in full awareness, meaning to say, “I attained the second absorption and the third absorption and the fourth absorption and the emptiness release and the signless release and the desireless release and the emptiness stillness and the signless stillness and the desireless stillness and the emptiness attainment and the signless attainment and the desireless attainment and the three true insights and the four applications of mindfulness and the four right efforts and the four foundations for supernormal power and the five spiritual faculties and the five spiritual powers and the seven factors of awakening and the noble eightfold path and the fruit of stream-entry and the fruit of once-returning and the fruit of non-returning and perfection …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.9.eng: and I’ve given up sensual desire …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.10.eng: and I’ve given up ill will …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.11.pli: moho ca me catto vanto mutto pahīno paṭinissaṭṭho ukkheṭito samukkheṭito, rāgā ca me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇaṁ dosā ca me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇaṁ mohā ca me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇanti vattukāmo paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjinti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa paṭivijānantassa āpatti pārājikassa;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.12.eng: if the listener does not understand, there is a serious offense when three conditions are fulfilled.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.13.eng: If he lies in full awareness, meaning to say, “I attained the third absorption and the fourth absorption …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.14.pli: mohā ca me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇaṁ paṭhamañca jhānaṁ samāpajjinti vattukāmo dutiyaṁ jhānaṁ samāpajjinti sampajānamusā bhaṇantassa paṭivijānantassa āpatti pārājikassa;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.15.eng: if the listener does not understand, he commits a serious offense when three conditions are fulfilled.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.16.pli: Tīhākārehi …pe… sattahākārehi mohā ca me cittaṁ vinīvaraṇaṁ paṭhamañca jhānaṁ dutiyañca jhānaṁ tatiyañca jhānaṁ catutthañca jhānaṁ …pe…
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.16.eng: If he lies in full awareness, meaning to say, “My mind is free from the hindrance of confusion and I attained the first absorption and the second absorption and the third absorption and the fourth absorption …

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.20.eng: while lying, he knows he is lying;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.21.eng: after he has lied, he knows he has lied;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.22.eng: he misrepresents his view of what is true;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.23.eng: he misrepresents his belief of what is true;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.24.eng: he misrepresents his acceptance of what is true;

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.25.eng: he misrepresents his sentiment of what is true.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.26.eng: The section based on all items with a speech extension is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.27.eng: The successive permutation series with a speech extension is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:5.4.28.eng: The exposition of the section on “meaning to say” is finished.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.1.1.pli: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu adhimānena aññaṁ byākāsi.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.1.1.eng: At one time a monk declared final knowledge because of overestimation.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.1.3.eng: “The Buddha has laid down a training rule.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.1.4.pli: Kacci nu kho ahaṁ pārājikaṁ āpattiṁ āpanno”ti?
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.1.4.eng: Could it be that I’ve committed an offense entailing expulsion?”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.2.1.pli: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu paṇidhāya araññe viharati—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.2.1.eng: At one time a monk lived in the wilderness

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.2.2.eng: because he wanted people to esteem him.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.2.6.pli: Na ca, bhikkhave, paṇidhāya araññe vatthabbaṁ. Yo vaseyya, āpatti dukkaṭassā”ti.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.2.6.eng: But, monks, you should not live in the wilderness because of a wish. If you do, you commit an offense of wrong conduct.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.2.7.pli: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu paṇidhāya piṇḍāya carati—
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.2.7.eng: At one time a monk was walking for almsfood

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.2.8.eng: because he wanted people to esteem him.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.2.12.pli: Na ca, bhikkhave, paṇidhāya piṇḍāya caritabbaṁ. Yo careyya, āpatti dukkaṭassā”ti.
pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.2.12.eng: But, monks, you should not walk for almsfood because of a wish. If you do, you commit an offense of wrong conduct.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.3.1.pli: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu aññataraṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.3.2.eng: “Those who are pupils of our preceptor are all perfected ones.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.3.5.eng: “I wanted to make a claim, sir.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.3.8.pli: Tena kho pana samayena aññataro bhikkhu aññataraṁ bhikkhuṁ etadavoca—

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.3.9.eng: “Those who are pupils of our preceptor all have great supernormal power.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.3.12.eng: “I wanted to make a claim, sir.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.7.3.eng: He was the one who lived in that dwelling.

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.7.6.eng: “I wanted to make a claim, sir.”

pli-tv-bu-vb-pj4:8.7.14.eng: “I wanted to make a claim, sir.”
